evals.prompt: Migrate this MatrixScan code from BarcodeBatch to BarcodeAr. The visual feedback should be circle highlights on each tracked barcode (matching the existing Dot overlay style). File: MigrationSimpleSample.tsx
evals.expected_output: The skill reads migration.md, replaces all BarcodeBatch* symbols with BarcodeAr equivalents, replaces DataCaptureView with BarcodeArView, wires a BarcodeArCircleHighlight provider with the Dot preset, switches dataCaptureContext.setMode to addMode, preserves the results state map and camera lifecycle, and calls view.start() in the BarcodeArView ref callback.

assertions.text: BarcodeBatch is NOT present in the output file
BarcodeBatchBasicOverlay is NOT present in the output file
BarcodeBatchSettings is NOT present in the output file
BarcodeBatchSession is NOT present in the output file
BarcodeAr is present in the output file
BarcodeArView is present in the output file
BarcodeArCircleHighlight is present in the output file
dataCaptureContext.addMode( is present in the output file
dataCaptureContext.setMode( is NOT present in the output file
DataCaptureView is NOT present in the output file
view.addOverlay( is NOT present in the output file
The results state and setResults setter are preserved (semantic)
camera.switchToDesiredState( is preserved in the output (camera lifecycle unchanged) (semantic)
view.start() is invoked inside the BarcodeArView ref callback (semantic)
The import from scandit-react-native-datacapture-barcode does not include any BarcodeBatch symbols (semantic)

evals.prompt: Migrate this MatrixScan code from BarcodeBatch to BarcodeAr. The bubbles above each barcode should become BarcodeAr annotations. Files: MigrationBubblesSampleScanPage.tsx and MigrationBubblesSampleARView.tsx
evals.expected_output: The skill reads migration.md, replaces all BarcodeBatch* symbols with BarcodeAr equivalents, replaces DataCaptureView with BarcodeArView, wires a BarcodeArAnnotationProvider that uses either BarcodeArCustomAnnotation (wrapping the ARView component) or BarcodeArInfoAnnotation, removes the manual getQuadrilateralWidth distance heuristic, migrates the lifecycle to view.start()/view.stop(), and removes the advanced overlay setup.

assertions.text: BarcodeBatch is NOT present in the output file
BarcodeBatchAdvancedOverlay is NOT present in the output file
BarcodeBatchBasicOverlay is NOT present in the output file
BarcodeBatchSettings is NOT present in the output file
BarcodeArAnnotationProvider is present in the output file
BarcodeArView is present in the output file
at least one of BarcodeArCustomAnnotation or BarcodeArInfoAnnotation is present in the output file (semantic)
DataCaptureView is NOT present in the output file
view.addOverlay( is NOT present in the output file
dataCaptureContext.setMode( is NOT present in the output file
dataCaptureContext.addMode( is present in the output file
The ARView component is preserved or its content is rendered inside the annotation (semantic)
Anchor.TopCenter import or usage from the old overlay listener is replaced — no standalone anchorForTrackedBarcode method remains (semantic)
getQuadrilateralWidth is NOT present in the output file
viewQuadrilateralForFrameQuadrilateral is NOT present in the output file
view.start() is invoked in the BarcodeArView ref callback (semantic)
view.stop() or viewRef.current?.stop() is used to stop scanning in place of barcodeBatch.isEnabled = false (semantic)
